titleStmt.title: Zine Bakery: exploring zines for DH research, methods training, and scholarly communication

author.affiliation: Scholars' Lab, University of Virginia, U.S.A

keywords.term: curricular and pedagogical development and analysis
I plan to attend the conference on-site in Lisbon

body.p: This poster familiarizes digital humanists with:

list.item: what zines can make possible for the digital humanities

body.p: by introducing the Zine Bakery (ZineBakery.com) scholarly project .

Zines are a media form often pictured as their late 20th-century format of self-designed and -published, collaged, xerox replicated, and freely distributed paper booklets on topics including subcultures, leftist politics, and personal essays. Today, creators make "zines" that vary widely in format, including 100+ page tiny books and no-print-version creative websites. Through a zine typology visualization, analysis of zine bibliographic features and creator goals, and showcase of exemplar DH zines, this poster demonstrates zines are a welcoming, inexpensive, and effective format for do-it-yourself scholarly communication, friendly teaching of digital research methods, and public humanities outreach—as well as an opportunity for data analysis and other DH explorations.
The Zine Bakery project is a portal to a variety of such zine-inspired DH opportunities, including:

list.item: A public relational-database catalogue of zines with 375+ DHy zines and over 60 metadata fields per zine; over 40 of those zines are explicitly DH-focused, with another 110+ zines in immediately adjacent areas including feminist tech, coding tutorials, AI, and data science (not counting zines in other related areas such as libraries and archives, and social justice advocacy)
Info visualization of DHy zine covers and bibliographic metadata
Active research blog sharing the latest DH zine theory and practice, including the technical aspects of dataset-building, catalogue interface design, and info visualization
Digital preservation of precariously-online zine files

body.p: In addition to showcasing and explaining the above DH project methodologies, this poster offers:

list.item: Both digital and data-physicalization versions of the DHy zine database (an actual quilt with sewn features further visualizing and summarizing zine catalogue metadata)
QR code linking to a bibliography of free e-reading versions of DHy zines
Printed-paper, example DHy zines attendees can take away for free
A limited number of DH zines themed reading card decks, distributed to attendees who share DHy zines not yet in my catalogue with me

body.p: This poster will interest DH center, makerspace, and library staff managing public spaces and events where zines could be taught or shared; teachers of digital methods seeking new ways to support student learning; scholars and students new to DH who'd benefit from seeing a Posner "how did they make that?" level of friendly documentation around a current DH project; and DH researchers looking for ways to better share their work with the public.

The Zine Bakery research catalogue's focus is on zines at the intersections of tech, culture, and justice, meaning its hundreds of zines largely overlap with conference themes, including digital humanities; public, crowdsourced, and citizen humanities; social justice, equity, diversity, inclusion in academia and around digital methods and projects; archives and digital preservation; ethics of humanities AI; DH pedagogy; technical method teaching; humanities approaches to climate sustainability; and social justice advocacy.

listBibl.head: Bibliography
listBibl.bibl: Fox, Violet B. et al. "The zine librarians code of ethics" web resource and zine. November 2015. https://www.zinelibraries.info/code-of-ethics-1115-web-version/.
Freedman, Jenna, et al. Zine Union Catalogue web resource . https://zinecat.org.
Freedman, Jenna, Filipa Calado, Miranda Johnson. Text Analysis With A Zine Corpus zine. 2022. https://archive.org/details/text-analysis-with-a-zine-corpus/page/n7/mode/2up.
Nemergut, Nicole Acosta. Teaching with Zines zine. 2018. https://github.com/zinecat/zinecat.org/blob/master/Documents/Teaching%20with%20Zines%20-%20Acosta.pdf.
Posner, Miriam. "How did they make that?" Miriam Posner's Blog post. August 2013. https://miriamposner.com/blog/how-did-they-make-that/.
Sahagian, Jacqui. "Zine-making as Critical DH Pedagogy". Scholars' Lab scholarly blog post, published January 14, 2022. https://scholarslab.lib.virginia.edu/blog/workshop-zine-translation/.
Stevens, Amanda, et al. Zine Subject Thesaurus web resource. https://anchorarchive.org/subject-thesaurus.

Visconti, Amanda Wyatt. “Book Adjacent: Database & Makerspace Prototypes Repairing Book-Centric Citation Bias in DH Working Libraries”. DH+Lib special issue on “Making Research Tactile: Critical Making and Data Physicalization in Digital Humanities”, Spring 2024. https://dhandlib.org/2024/04/29/book-adjacent-database-makerspace-prototypes-repairing-book-centric-citation-bias-in-dh-working-libraries/.

---. "Organized futures: speculative infrastructure design for more just and joyful scholarly community”. Journal of Electronic Publishing special issue "On Gathering: exploring collective and embodied modes of scholarly communication and publishing". Forthcoming Fall 2024.

---. "Themed Reading Card Decks". In Play with Your Data zine, part of DH+Lib journal special issue on “Making Research Tactile: Critical Making and Data Physicalization in Digital Humanities”, Spring 2024. https://hcommons.org/deposits/item/hc:65563.

---. Zine Bakery. Digital humanities scholarly project, research blog, data visualizations, zine online and public space distros, zine publication, and relational database catalogue. Last updated 2024. ZineBakery.com. Project's research blogging includes:

Visconti, Amanda Wyatt; Quinn Dombrowski; Claudia Berger. “#DHmakes: Baking Craft into DH Discourse”. Korean Journal of Digital Humanities, 1(1), 73-108, 2024. https://accesson.kr/kjdh/v.1/1/73/43507.
Wrekk, Alex. Stolen sharpie revolution: a DIY resource for zines and zine culture zine. 6th edition, 2020. https://stolensharpierevolution.org/.
